body_txt: This is an Omnipresent Trope : it can be assumed to exist in every medium unless otherwise stated. In most forms, the assumption is that women are delicate, suited for nurturing, and/or prone to focusing on their physical appearance , making them less suitable for active roles requiring strength and toughness. Women tend to be physically smaller than men and possess less musculature, and these things, amongst others, have created the assumption that female individuals are not as "hardy" as male individuals. The Stereotype takes two forms: Nearly every medium plays around with this in some way. There are some tropes (for example, Stay in the Kitchen ) which take it almost to the point of parody, whereas other tropes (such as Action Girl ) play it anything but straight. From there, the combinations tend to get tricky. Some tropes treat it ironically ( Waif-Fu ) whereas others reinforce it by having the more masculine of two female characters be the disposable one. Under this belief, it's also commonly assumed that beauty is Woman's "hat" : what female characters naturally lack in power or capability they make up for in appearance. In modern culture, it is nigh-impossible to not have some sort of variation of this trope. Only in some hypothetical, gender-neutral civilization would that be possible, and that does not describe the world as it exists today. Even if unintended by the author , the audience will take whatever tropes are used and add meaning to them. A girl who puts up a fight alongside her male colleagues will still be considered an "aversion" of this trope whereas a girl who is meek and prefers to sit down and talk will be a straight example. Lastly, note that the word we chose to define this trope is "delicate". That word is not just about the woman's physical or emotional toughness. Delicate also means "precious, careful, deliberate, thorough", which are also stereotypically feminine traits (see Women Are Wiser and Men Use Violence, Women Use Communication ). Delicate is the word which best sums up everything this trope is about. Please keep that in mind when adding/editing the subtropes. We intentionally chose a polysemic word for this trope because Tropes Are Flexible , and like all stereotypes, this one is an Evolving Trope and changes a LOT as works of media attempt to play it up or play it down . A Sister Trope to Men Are Tough . Might be a result of Gender Incompetence . No Examples, Please . This page should be used as nothing except a listing of tropes which play with this in some way.
